summary: Comprehensive backend + frontend e2e testing of Vesper Stremio addon TV-box app. All 13 backend pytest tests pass (root, suggested, install/list/remove, catalog with search & cache, meta aggregator, streams aggregator, invalid URL error). Frontend Home renders hero billboard with Geist sans-serif title, neon-blue accents, side nav, fullscreen button, Play button initial focus + blue glow ring, 8 live Cinemeta shelves and 72 real movie posters. D-pad spatial focus works (ArrowDown jumps from Play into first poster, ArrowRight moves between posters). Sources page: Add by URL toggles OnScreenKeyboard, Cinemeta/OpenSubtitles/WatchHub suggested cards visible, install of WatchHub shows success toast, removal works. Detail page renders 'The Wizard of Oz' meta with backdrop and stream picker. Search renders OSK. Player attaches HLS.js to .m3u8 (test stream displayed expected codec error — not a Vesper bug).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:248 — AddonOut id falls back to r['_id_str'] but the projection {'_id': 0} doesn't strip the custom '_id_str' (we set it). It works because every install path stamps _id_str=manifest.id, but it's redundant with addon_id. Prefer using r['addon_id'] directly.

server.py:475 CORS allow_origins='*' AND allow_credentials=True is technically invalid per CORS spec (browsers reject credentials with wildcard). Not a runtime issue today since frontend doesn't send credentials, but worth tightening.
Player.jsx uses HLS.js which is correct for .m3u8; for raw mp4/direct urls a fallback to native <video src> should be confirmed (not exercised in this run).

lib/api.js resolvePoster has buggy ternary precedence: `item?.poster || item?.posterUrl || item?.posterShape === 'landscape' ? item?.poster : item?.poster || null` — essentially always returns item.poster. Logic should be parenthesised, but currently does no harm because all paths return poster.
Frontend never displays a global error boundary — runtime errors in any page would white-screen the TV box; recommend adding ErrorBoundary.
test_credentials: N/A - single-user mode (DEFAULT_USER='default' on backend). No auth implemented.
seed_data_creation: Cinemeta addon installed during testing (state persists in MongoDB 'addons' collection for user_id=default). WatchHub installed then removed during sources page test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: App is at https://rebrand-app-5.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend tests at /app/backend/tests/test_vesper_api.py — they cleanup pre-existing addons then install Cinemeta + WatchHub. Cinemeta typically remains installed after tests. If retesting frontend Home with live shelves, ensure Cinemeta is installed first via POST /api/addons/install. Public addons reachable from k8s env. Player uses HLS.js (confirmed). OnScreenKeyboard widget uses internal state — global keyboard.type() does NOT update its value (only on-screen button clicks do); for testing search flow, click letter buttons or paste into input directly.
rca of the issue: No bugs found requiring fixing. The Player's 'manifestIncompatibleCodecsError' was caused by the test stream (test-streams.mux.dev/x36xhzz) using a codec not supported in headless Chromium — this is expected HLS.js behavior, not a Vesper bug; HLS.js attached and parsed the manifest successfully which is what was being verified.
